headline: KKR, Taiyo agree to privatization; KKR enters into agreements with DIC, Kowa
summary: KKR (KKR) and Taiyo Holdings announced that KJ005 Co., Ltd., an entity owned by investment funds managed by KKR, intends to make a tender offer to acquire all the common shares of Taiyo Holdings. Taiyo Holdings’ Board of Directors has resolved to support the Tender Offer. In addition, DIC Corporation, Taiyo Holdings’ largest shareholder, Kowa Co., Ltd., an asset management company affiliated with Taiyo Holdings’ founding family, and funds managed by Oasis Management Company Ltd. have each entere

headline: Does Blackstone's Fundraising Strength Anchor Its Growth Story?
summary: BX's fundraising muscle drove AUM to $1.3T, boosting fee-based earnings stability and reinforcing a powerful investment and growth flywheel.

headline: KKR Weighs Defense Data Centre Deal Aviation Exit And Consumer Pivot
summary: KKR (NYSE:KKR) is in exclusive talks with the US Army to co-develop a large data centre on a military base to support defense AI infrastructure. The firm is reported to be progressing on a potential sale of Atlantic Aviation. KKR is also part of a bidding group interested in acquiring Nestle's water business. For investors tracking NYSE:KKR, these moves sit at the intersection of infrastructure, defense technology, aviation services, and consumer products. The proposed US Army data centre...

headline: KKR Moves To Take Taiyo Private In High-Premium Buyout Bet
summary: KKR to privatize Taiyo Holdings in a strategic tender offer to accelerate long-term growth in electronics and pharmaceutical sectors.
